name: Perfect Buttermilk Biscuit
recipeInstructions:
1. Place shortening in a small plastic food bag.
2. Flatten the shortening between plastic sheets and return to freezer.
3. Remove shortening from freezer and cut into small bits, about 1/4-inch square.
4. Drop shortening bits into a bowl of dry ingredients and mix with fingertips, occasionally tossing flour mixture so you touch all particles of shortening with flour.
5. When the mixture has bits of flour-covered shortening throughout, add buttermilk using a fork, lightly stirring to mix with dry ingredients.
6. Cover board or surface with dusting of flour.
7. Gather sticky mass of dough and place on floured surface.
8. Dust hands with flour and gently knead dough, adding enough flour only to make dough manageable.
9. Pat dough into round 1/2-inch thick using a floured rolling pin.
10. Using a 2-inch cutter, cut out biscuits and arrange touching each other in the center of a greased baking sheet.
11. Place on middle rack of a 425-degree oven and bake for 12 minutes, or until lightly golden.
12. Remove from oven and serve hot or warm. Makes 19 (2-inch round) biscuits.
